title: Citrusy Crumbly Croatian Citrus Crumble
description: A lazy-sunday treat featuring a citrusy crumbly topping with star anise and sous-vide technique for egg-free-friendly dessert.

ingredients.name: Sour Cherries (or any sour fruit of choice)
Star Anise (grounded)
ingredients.notes: For unique citrusy aroma and taste

ingredients.name: Saffron Threads
ingredients.notes: For color and depth of flavor

instructions: Place sour cherries, lemon zest, star anise, saffron threads, vanilla bean, and lemon juice in a large zip-top bag. Seal tightly and marinate at room temperature for about 2 hours.
Preheat your sous-vide machine to 71°C (160°F) and place the marinade-filled bag in it for about 30 minutes to tenderize the fruit while infusing flavors.
Transfer the mixture to a large pot, add granulated sugar and bring to a boil over medium heat. Stir until the sugar dissolves completely, then remove from heat.
Add almond meal or flour, vegetable oil, and lemon zest to the pot. Mix well until you achieve a crumbly texture, being careful not to overmix as it might affect the consistency of the topping.
Transfer the warm mixture to a clean surface, let it cool slightly. Gently fold in any additional ingredients like seeds or nuts for an extra burst of flavor if desired.
Preheat your oven to 150°C (300°F). Spoon the crumbly mixture over the fruit and spread evenly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until golden brown. Allow it to cool slightly before serving garnished with extra lemon zest if desired.
